steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The researcher claims some lakes have seen a decline in ice duration between the early 1980s and the mid-2000s. To cite a "typical example," the correct choice must show a single lake with fewer days of ice in 2005–06 than in 1980–81.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'both Spirit Lake and Kitusjärvi, which had more than 120 days of ice in the winter of 2005–06': Reports only 2005–06 data — no comparison to the 1980s, so it cannot show a decline. ✗
  • Choice B — 'Kitusjärvi, which had fewer days of ice in the winter of 2005–06 than it did in the winter of 1980–81': Compares both time periods and shows a decrease — exactly the pattern the researcher describes. ✓
  • Choice C — 'Spirit Lake, which had more days of ice in the winter of 2005–06 than it did in the winter of 1980–81': Shows an increase, the opposite of the claimed decline. ✗
  • Choice D — 'both Spirit Lake and Kitusjärvi, which had fewer than 170 days of ice in the winter of 1980–81': Reports only 1980–81 data — no comparison to the mid-2000s. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
